steps.title: Identify the criterion for tall
steps.content: A perennial flower is classified as tall if its height exceeds 100 centimeters. To represent a tall species, an inequality must include at least some possible heights where $h \gt  100$.

steps.title: Find the range that includes heights greater than 100
steps.content:
Check the maximum value in each inequality:
  • Choice A — $86 \lt h \lt 86$ impossible
  • Choice B — $86 \lt h \lt 89$ all values are less than 100
  • Choice C — $86 \lt h \lt 100$ all values satisfy $h \lt 100$
  • Choice D — $86 \lt h \lt 154$ includes values greater than 100 (from 100 to 154)
Only the matching choice's range permits tall plants. D is correct.
